description_long: This is a classic pipe wrench featuring a robust, dark metallic head with an adjustable jaw mechanism and a long, cylindrical wooden handle. The head of the wrench is composed of two main parts: a fixed lower jaw and an upper jaw that can be adjusted using a knurled nut and gear system. The metal head shows intricate mechanical details, including teeth for gripping pipes and a visible adjustment screw. The wooden handle is smooth, slightly tapered, and appears well-worn, suggesting frequent use. The overall finish of the metal is matte with some minor surface irregularities, while the wood displays a natural grain and a slightly glossy patina. The wrench is utilitarian in design, suitable for heavy-duty tasks in urban maintenance or repair settings.
description: A heavy-duty pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable head and a smooth, worn wooden handle. The tool features detailed mechanical parts and a utilitarian, well-used appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the adjustable jaws and the gear mechanism of the wrench head.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the wrench, the wooden handle, and the detailed jaw adjustment system.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the wooden handle and the back of the wrench head.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile displaying the other side of the jaw and handle, with clear visibility of the adjustment screw.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Minor wear marks visible on the wooden handle; metal head appears slightly worn but no significant rust or corrosion.
